Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First let me understand the standard Hire to Retire process 1 Post Job Ad 2 Screen Candidates 3 Conduct Interviews 4 Make Hiring Decision 5 Onboard Employee 6 Add to Payroll 7 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is typically a sequential process with some possible variations for example screening before interviewing decision after interviewing onboarding before payroll

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets analyze Model 1 Nodes Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Post before Screen good Screen before Decide problematic decision usually comes after interview Screen before Interview good Decide before Onboard good

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But theres no edge between Interview and Decide

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means Decide can happen before Interview or in parallel with Interview since theres no edge Interview to Decide or Decide to Interview

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But Decide to Onboard means once Decide happens Onboard can happen even if Interview hasnt happened yet

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So in Model 1 you can make a hiring decision before conducting interviews or concurrently This is a major anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interview has incoming from Screen but no outgoing edges So Interview is a sink in terms of ordering

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: There is no constraint that Interview before Onboard Since Onboard depends on Decide and Decide doesnt depend on Interview Onboard can happen without Interview

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means you can onboard an employee before interviewing them which is a severe anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: There is no constraint that Interview must happen before Close No relation between Interview and Close So Close could happen before Interview

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So Model 1 anomalies 1 Interview and Decide are unordered Decision can be made before or without interviews being completed 2 Interview has no outgoing edges so it doesnt gate anything 3 The process can complete Close without Interview being done or Interview can happen after Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now Model 2 Post Screen Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close skip

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Post to Screen and Post to Interview But Screen and Interview are unordered So you could interview before screening Thats an anomaly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Interview to Decide Good decision after interview

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: loop onboarding means execute Onboard then either exit or execute skip and then Onboard again So you can onboard multiple times

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: This might represent a rework loop if onboarding was not complete but it is unusual

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: xor payroll means either Add to Payroll or skip So payroll is optional

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: You can close the case without adding to payroll Thats an anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Screen has no outgoing edges So Screen does not lead to anything You could make a decision without screening or screen after everything else

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So Model 2 anomalies 1 Screen is disconnected from the main flow 2 Screen and Interview are unordered so you can interview before screening 3 Onboarding is in a loop allowing repeated onboarding 4 Payroll is optional 5 You can decide without screening because Screen is unrelated to Decide

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Both models have a dangling activity Model 1 has Interview dangling Model 2 has Screen dangling

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: In Model 1 you can hire someone without interviewing them In Model 2 you can interview without screening first and may skip payroll

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Making a hiring decision without interviews is probably more severe than interviewing without screening

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 at least has Interview before Decide which is crucial

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 2 preserves the Interview to Decide to Onboard to Payroll to Close chain though with loop and xor Model 1 breaks the chain by omitting Interview to Decide

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I think the answer should be that Model 2 is closer to normative because the main sequence Interview to Decide to Onboard to Close is preserved whereas Model 1 breaks the fundamental sequence by allowing Decide before Interview

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 2 more closely aligns with the normative process because it preserves the critical logical dependency that a hiring decision must be preceded by an interview Model 1 violates this fundamental constraint by allowing the decision to be made before or independently of interviews
